The 'muddy point' assignment and check-in surveys show some attempt at reflection, but they appear only once or twice rather than being distributed throughout the course. Most quiz instructions suggest keeping notes about questions, but this is informal guidance rather than structured reflection with instructor feedback. There is no clear evidence that students receive formative feedback specifically on their reflection activities, and the reflection opportunities don't explicitly help students connect effort to results or plan for improvement. The course approaches the standard but does not fully meet it due to the limited number and distribution of reflection opportunities.

missing_items: No anonymous feedback survey found near the end of the course
No learner feedback mechanism identified in final module
No course evaluation tool visible in provided content
No reference to end-of-course feedback opportunity in syllabus or closing materials
rubric_findings.suggested_fix: Add an anonymous survey tool (such as a Canvas survey or external form like Google Forms with anonymous response settings) in the final module or week of the course. Include clear instructions inviting students to provide feedback on course design, content effectiveness, learning activities, workload, and overall experience. Ensure the survey is positioned after students have experienced the majority of course content but before final grades are submitted. Reference this feedback opportunity in the syllabus under course policies or evaluation procedures.
rubric_findings.reasoning: The provided course content includes extensive materials from Units 1-13, covering the welcome module, multiple content units, exam summaries, and various course policies and resources. Despite this comprehensive view, there is no evidence of an anonymous feedback survey or mechanism for students to provide course feedback to the instructor. The content shows check-in references in passing (page g3e75789416693cf1580adf313395b45c mentions 'Check-In and Feedback surveys, occur at various times throughout the semester'), but no actual survey instrument, instructions for accessing feedback tools, or specific invitation to provide anonymous feedback is visible. The course appears to conclude with Exam 2 materials, but no end-of-course feedback opportunity is documented in the final module content provided. The context note indicates content was truncated, but given the extensive materials provided covering beginning through end of course, and the specific absence of any feedback survey in the closing materials, this element cannot be confirmed as present and must be rated as not_evaluable rather than making assumptions about content not provided.
